mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% in low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer and investment demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods show positive 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index above 0.5% reflects accelerating supply-side momentum that triggers short-term equity buying pressure over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative returns in > 55% of triggered instances during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 2.5% triggers expectations of central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 65% of high-inflation trigger months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and a stable or falling unemployment rate indicates labor market tightening that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Divergence periods yield negative returns in > 50% of cases, or joint signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate demand that translates into elevated corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency < 50% in high-GDP quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production below -0.3% signals weakening manufacturing output that reduces near-term earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances during late-cycle phases.
